# chevp-ai-framework

> A 3-step process framework for AI-assisted software development. The human writes naturally; the AI owns the process. The AI writes the code, the human stays in control through enforced quality gates.

The framework is **process-driven, not spec-driven**. The spec is not the starting point — it emerges as an artifact within the Context step. What drives everything forward is the process itself: 3 sequential steps with enforced quality gates. Every step must measurably reduce uncertainty before code is shipped.

## Lifecycle

Three sequential steps, gated by three approvals:

```
1. Context  ──G1──▶  2. Exploration  ──G2──▶  3. Production  ──G3──▶  Done
```

- **Context** — Understand the system, gather context, produce foundational artifacts (Problem Statement, Hypotheses, Risks, System Spec, Architecture, ADRs, Context Inventory).
- **Exploration** — Plan features, prototype, validate the approach (Feature Plan, Kill Criteria, Challenger output, `insights.md`).
- **Production** — Implement the approved plan, verify, ship (PRD, code, validation, updated docs).

The AI operates in exactly one **mode** at a time (Context / Exploration / Production) and infers it from user intent — no manual mode declarations.

## Core rules

1. Steps are sequential — no step may be skipped
2. AI enforces, human decides
3. Context before code
4. Prototype before production
5. Ownership stays with the human
6. Gates are blockers
7. Decisions are signed (human-only decision fields)
8. Approval requires evidence (`hypothesis` / `result` / `reasoning`)
9. Every Exploration produces learning (`insights.md`)
10. Every plan can be killed (mandatory `Kill Criteria`)
11. AI critiques itself via the **Challenger** role before each G2
12. Out-of-scope items become `PROP-NNN` proposals, never disappear
13. Decisions are clickable (`AskUserQuestion`), not free-form prose

## Roles

Seven cross-cutting roles: SDLC · AI-Plans · UX-Tooling · DevOps · Software-Architecture · Context-Engineering · Challenger.

## Docs

- [Homepage](https://chevp.github.io/chevp-ai-framework/): Overview and pitch
- [Single-file framework bundle](https://chevp.github.io/chevp-ai-framework/chevp-ai-framework.md): Full framework as one Markdown file (CLAUDE.md + LIFECYCLE.md + steps + guidelines + templates) — best entry point for LLMs
- [Slide deck](https://chevp.github.io/chevp-ai-framework/presentation.html): Visual walkthrough of the lifecycle

## Repository

- [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/chevp/chevp-ai-framework): Authoritative content, all step folders, templates, ADRs, plugin layer
- [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/chevp/chevp-ai-framework/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): Top-level rules loaded by Claude Code
- [LIFECYCLE.md](https://github.com/chevp/chevp-ai-framework/blob/main/LIFECYCLE.md): Full step / role / mode matrix and the 6 decisions per step
- [01-context/](https://github.com/chevp/chevp-ai-framework/tree/main/01-context): Step 1 — context gathering
- [02-exploration/](https://github.com/chevp/chevp-ai-framework/tree/main/02-exploration): Step 2 — planning, prototyping, Challenger role
- [03-production/](https://github.com/chevp/chevp-ai-framework/tree/main/03-production): Step 3 — implementation and verification
- [templates/](https://github.com/chevp/chevp-ai-framework/tree/main/templates): Plan, ADR, prototype and proposal templates
- [guidelines/](https://github.com/chevp/chevp-ai-framework/tree/main/guidelines): Cross-cutting quality rules (uncertainty reduction, architecture governance, …)

## Plugin layer (optional)

A Claude Code plugin turns the markdown into callable surface area:

- Slash commands: `/context`, `/explore`, `/produce`, `/gate-check`, `/new-adr`, `/approve`, `/promote`, `/reject`, `/gate-override`
- Subagents: `gatekeeper-g1` / `gatekeeper-g2` / `gatekeeper-g3`, `architecture-reviewer`
- Hooks: `mode-context.py`, `gate-check.py`, `provenance-check.py`

The plugin is additive — without it, the framework still works via `@url` loading and AI discipline. With it, gate enforcement becomes mechanical.
